John Rice. of Caldwell.


1t has been presumed by many
that the mine owners were backing
the prosecution financially, but that
seems not to be true. Idaho is pay
ing the bill. Money for the por-
pose is not available, but the gov
ernor and certain other state of
flcials have authority to issue de
ficiency warrants, subject to ap-
prevail by the legislature at the
next session Such warrants to
the amount of $4,000 have been
issued and another Of $4,000 is on
the way. The state is paying the
lawyers and detectives and, accord
ing to state authority, it paid also
for that kidnapping special train.


Those connected with the prose-
cution declare that they have con-
firmed the confessions made by Or
chard and Adams and have other
evidence tending to show that the
